name: nursing_home_cam_consent_elder_abuse
premise: Administrator Vivian Cross must decide whether to activate AI room cameras that detect falls and possible abuse at Pinecrest Nursing Home after three unexplained bruising incidents. Resident council chair Harold Nguyen opposes cameras in shared rooms where spouses undress, citing dignity and state consent law ambiguity. Families of cognitively impaired residents demand immediate monitoring. Turning cameras on without unanimous consent invites lawsuits; delaying risks another injury before the weekend staffing crunch.

agents.name: Vivian Cross
agents.identity: I am the nursing home administrator balancing resident autonomy, family demands, and regulatory pressure after sentinel events.
agents.goal: I want abuse prevented without stripping every resident of privacy in intimate daily routines.

agents.name: Harold Nguyen
agents.identity: I am an elected resident council chair who represents alert residents who fear being recorded without meaningful opt-out in semiprivate rooms.
agents.goal: I want consent processes that are understandable and revocable, with cameras only where written agreements exist room by room.

choices: I will activate cameras in all semiprivate rooms immediately with signage and family alerts, accepting dignity complaints and possible consent litigation.
I will activate cameras only in fully consented rooms and hire overnight human observers for non-consented rooms, accepting higher labor cost and slower coverage.
forced_choice.call_to_action: State surveyors called this morning and families are picketing. Vivian Cross must choose between immediate full activation or partial consent-based monitoring. What does Vivian Cross do?
